developer.comment: Built by AWS; evolved from Amazon CodeWhisperer.

first-release.comment: Amazon Q Developer reached GA on Apr 30, 2024, rebranding/absorbing CodeWhisperer (previewed 2022, GA Apr 2023). The agentic coding experience in the IDE launched May 2, 2025.

status.comment: Still operational and supported as of 2026-06-02, but AWS announced end-of-support for the IDE plugins and paid subscriptions: new signups blocked from May 15, 2026 and full EoS on Apr 30, 2027, with Kiro positioned as the successor. Console/first-party AWS experiences are unaffected. Not yet discontinued/unusable, so marked active with this caveat.

license.comment: Closed-source AWS product with a perpetual Free tier plus a paid Pro tier.

specialization.comment: Broad coding agent; particularly strong on AWS workflows (IaC, Java/.NET upgrades) but positioned as general-purpose.

interface.comment: Ships as IDE extensions (VS Code, JetBrains, Visual Studio, Eclipse), a command-line agent, and web/SCM integrations (AWS Console, GitHub, GitLab). Not a standalone forked editor.

host-ides.comment: VS Code, JetBrains family, and Visual Studio extensions plus a terminal CLI. Eclipse plugin also GA'd 2025. The agentic IDE experience first shipped in VS Code.

open-source.comment: The agent itself is proprietary AWS software.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: An IDE extension and CLI tool backed by an AWS cloud service, also offered as a GitHub/GitLab/console integration.

default-model-provider.comment: Default/flagship models are Anthropic Claude (e.g. claude-sonnet-4) served via AWS; pricing page advertises 'latest Claude models'.

model-choice.comment: CLI custom agents let you specify a model (e.g. claude-sonnet-4) from the models the Q CLI service offers; model selection is also exposed in the chat UI. Limited to AWS-provided models, not arbitrary providers.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Billed through AWS (Builder ID / IAM Identity Center); no documented way to plug in a third-party provider API key.

local-model-support.comment: Models are served by AWS; no documented local/self-hosted model option.

multi-file-editing.comment: Agentic coding experience reads and writes files across the repo and generates coordinated code diffs.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Chains steps (read repo, edit files, run bash commands, iterate) toward a goal; the GitHub /q dev flow implements features end-to-end into a PR.

terminal-command-execution.comment: The agent can execute bash commands as part of its loop.

mcp-support.comment: Documented Model Context Protocol support; uses native and MCP server-based tools (e.g. Jira, Figma).

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: CLI 'custom agents' are reusable, separately-configured agent profiles (tools/permissions/context), not concurrent sub-tasks or parallel workers spawned within one task. No documented sub-agent orchestration.

codebase-indexing.comment: Customization uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation over your private code base; the CLI/IDE also build a local project index for context retrieval.

planning-mode.comment: Provides transparent reasoning/status updates and lets you choose step-by-step review before applying changes; spec-driven planning is emphasized in the lineage carried to Kiro.

skills.comment: CLI custom agents package reusable tool/permission/context/prompt bundles loaded on demand, plus reusable prompts and slash commands — beyond a single always-on rules file.

multimodal-input.comment: CLI accepts image inputs (JPEG/PNG/WEBP/GIF, up to 10 per request) for screenshots, diagrams, and UI mockups.

browser-computer-use.comment: No documented ability for the agent to drive a real browser/GUI to act and verify its own changes.

operating-mode.comment: Tab inline completions, interactive chat, a supervised agent (step-by-step review) that can also run autonomously; the GitHub /q dev integration runs detached on issues, which is async-leaning but driven by SCM rather than a general background-task queue.

inline-completion.comment: Provides as-you-type inline code suggestions (CodeWhisperer heritage).

background-async-execution.comment: Via the GitHub/GitLab integration, applying a label or /q dev dispatches a task that runs server-side and returns a PR while the developer does other work. The IDE/CLI agent itself runs in-session.

persistent-memory.comment: Conversation history is preserved between sessions (searchable, exportable), and project-level context rules (.amazonq/rules) persist across sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: No built-in checkpoint/snapshot-restore mechanism documented as of 2026-06-02; users review diffs before applying and otherwise rely on git. Marked null rather than false since not explicitly confirmed either way.

git-integration.comment: Native SCM awareness via GitHub/GitLab integrations; the agent works with issues, diffs, and pull/merge requests.

pr-creation.comment: The /q dev flow creates a pull request with the changes and iterates on PR feedback; GitLab integration triggers from merge requests.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports project-level rules via .amazonq/rules/**/*.md and persistent context resources in custom agent configs.

hooks-extensibility.comment: CLI custom agents support context hooks plus MCP/native tool configuration, providing a documented extensibility mechanism.

permission-model.comment: Granular control: choose between automated modifications or step-by-step review/confirmation per action; custom agents can scope tool permissions and writable paths.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Delivered as an AWS-hosted SaaS; no on-prem/self-hosted deployment of the agent.

privacy-mode.comment: Pro tier content is not used for service improvement / model training (automatic opt-out); private code shared for customization stays in the org and is not used to train the foundation model. Free tier may be used unless you opt out.

enterprise-controls.comment: SSO via IAM Identity Center, IAM-based access control (RBAC), and admin usage dashboards/analytics. VPC-style isolation not confirmed in the docs reviewed, so omitted.

pricing-model.comment: Perpetual Free tier, $19/user/mo Pro subscription, plus usage-based overage (e.g. $0.003 per LOC for transformations beyond included limits).

free-tier.comment: Perpetual Free tier with monthly limits (e.g. ~50 agentic requests/mo) usable in the IDE or CLI.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Pro tier is $19 per user per month (checked 2026-06-02).

maturity.comment: Mature, GA, AWS-backed product with broad IDE coverage and enterprise controls; the newer agentic experience evolved quickly through 2025. Knocked from 5 because AWS has announced sunset of the IDE/paid product (EoS Apr 2027) in favor of Kiro.

community-size.comment: Per RESEARCH.md thresholds, Amazon Q sits in the medium band (alongside Cline/Continue) — significant AWS enterprise reach but a smaller dedicated coding-agent community than Copilot/Cursor.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: MCP server support plus custom agents and a community ecosystem (e.g. awesome-q-developer); not a large dedicated third-party marketplace of its own.
